label: Elasticsearch
description: Connect to an Elasticsearch cluster to query indices, ingest documents, and run full-text or vector searches.

form.title: Connect Elasticsearch
form.subtitle: Enter your cluster URL and credentials to let Anton query and ingest data.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste a cluster URL and an Elasticsearch API key. No role configuration needed — the key carries its own privileges. Recommended for most setups.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with an Elasticsearch API Key

API keys are scoped credentials tied to a specific cluster. They are simpler to rotate and audit than username/password pairs.

### Steps

1. Open **Kibana** → **Stack Management** → **Security** → **API Keys**.
2. Click **Create API key**.
3. Give it a name like `anton-key`.
4. Optionally restrict it to specific indices or privilege levels under **Restrict privileges** (recommended in production).
5. Click **Create API key**. Copy the key shown — it is displayed **only once**.
6. Paste your cluster URL (e.g. `https://my-cluster.es.io:9200`) into **Cluster URL** below.
7. Paste the key into **API Key** below.

### Notes

- For **Elastic Cloud**, the cluster URL is on the deployment overview page: **Cloud Console → Deployments → your deployment → Elasticsearch endpoint**.
- For self-hosted clusters, use the Elasticsearch HTTP endpoint (default port 9200). Include `https://` if TLS is enabled.
- If your cluster uses a self-signed certificate, ensure it is trusted by the system running Anton or disable certificate verification with caution.
- Rotate the key any time in Kibana without changing your username or password.

fields.name: cluster_url
fields.label: Cluster URL
fields.description: The full HTTP(S) endpoint of your Elasticsearch cluster, including port.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: The encoded API key value from Kibana → Stack Management → API Keys. Shown only once at creation.

methods.label: Username & Password
methods.description: Authenticate with an Elasticsearch username and password. Suitable for clusters with built-in users or native realm users configured via Kibana or the Elasticsearch security API.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Username & Password

Use an Elasticsearch native-realm user with appropriate privileges. Avoid using the `elastic` superuser in production — create a dedicated user instead.

### Create a dedicated user (recommended)

1. Open **Kibana** → **Stack Management** → **Security** → **Users**.
2. Click **Create user**.
3. Set a username (e.g. `anton-user`) and a strong password.
4. Under **Roles**, assign at least `read` and `monitor` on the indices Anton will access. Or use the built-in `viewer` role for read-only access.
5. Click **Create user**.

### Connect in Anton

1. Enter your cluster URL (e.g. `https://my-cluster.es.io:9200`) in **Cluster URL**.
2. Enter the username and password you created above.

### Notes

- For **Elastic Cloud**, the cluster URL is on the deployment overview: **Cloud Console → Deployments → your deployment → Elasticsearch endpoint**.
- The built-in `elastic` superuser password is set at cluster creation time. If you forgot it, reset it via the `elasticsearch-reset-password` CLI tool.
- For large teams or automated workflows, prefer the **API Key** method — keys are easier to rotate and scope without modifying user roles.

fields.name: cluster_url

fields.name: username
